personalInfo.name: Md. Toufiqul Islam
personalInfo.title: Software Engineer | Node.js, Golang, Docker, Kubernetes
personalInfo.bio: A software engineer with 6 years of professional experience working in high volume and fast paced environments. Skilled in creating scalable and impactful products using variety of technologies including Node.js, Go, Docker & kubernetes. Passionate about building robust systems, mentoring engineers, and optimizing performance at scale.

projects.title: Video Touch
projects.description: A Video Hosting & Streaming Platform with video transcoding, thumbnail generation, and on-demand streaming via CDN

projects.title: Deploybl
projects.description: Platform as a Service (PaaS) to simplify deployment and management of applications with automatic URL generation and support for any language

workExperience.companyIntro: RoBenDevs is a fast-growing company based in Bangladesh developing innovative solutions that meet the needs of today's ever-changing technological landscape.
keyResponsibilities: Leading the Full Stack Engineering team

workExperience.companyIntro: JIAK is a Singapore-based SaaS startup modernizing the F&B industry with an all-in-one platform for reservations, digital ordering, POS, and analytics—helping restaurants streamline operations and grow through smarter tech.
keyResponsibilities: Owned end-to-end development of core merchant services, including Digital Menu Management, real-time Waitlist Queuing, and automated merchant-facing Notifications, serving 50+ restaurants
Developed the core public-facing REST API to power the customer reservation platform
Executed strategic refactoring of legacy codebase to improve code reusability
Implemented asynchronous job queue using Redis and BullMQ for background tasks
Partnered with frontend and design teams to define clear API contracts

workExperience.companyIntro: 10 Minute School is Bangladesh's largest EdTech platform, offering free classes, tutorials, and quizzes for students (grades 1–12), university admissions, and skill development.
keyResponsibilities: Promoted through Software Engineer and Senior Software Engineer to Engineering Manager for consistent high performance and leadership
Rearchitected the caching strategy for the critical Central Routine API, slashing peak-hour p99 latency by over 85% to ensure a reliable user experience under high load

Led Live Video Streaming infrastructure for conducting live classes with real-time interactions. Serves around 30k students participating in different live classes and handling approximately 100k RPM during peak hours every day
Architected and deployed an in-house Video Processing Pipeline, reducing monthly operational costs by 75% while processing over 800 videos
Reduced cloud infrastructure costs by 60% through strategic optimization initiatives
Led a sub-team to develop central Learning Report service that improved user engagement by providing comprehensive weekly and monthly reports
Led end-to-end implementation of OpenTelemetry across microservices that enhanced system monitoring capabilities
Developed REST API for different microservices of 10MS Billing System, handling 10k+ transactions every day
Designed and led the development of an automated push notification system using event-driven Microservice Architecture
Implemented REST API for Exam Service, resulting in onboarding 140k new users
Collaborated with cross functional teams to design, develop, and deploy features to production
Mentored junior engineers & interns to build production ready services
Integrated AWS tools like S3, SNS, SQS and Lambda with the application

workExperience.companyIntro: Khamar-e is an agri-tech startup working towards a data-driven farming and supply chain ecosystem in Bangladesh
keyResponsibilities: Developed front-end and back-end of Health Tracker IOT device for tracking cattle activity

education.institution: Ahsanullah University of Science and Technology
education.degree: Bachelor of Science in Computer Science and Engineering
education.description: Undergraduate Thesis: Greenness Analysis of Dhaka City Using the NDVI MODIS Satellite Database

testimonials.name: Abu Sufian
testimonials.title: CTO
testimonials.feedback:
As the CTO at JIAK, I had the opportunity to work closely with Md Toufiqul Islam, and he consistently proved to be one of the most grounded, dependable, and high-impact backend engineers on the team. During our crucial 0→1 phase, Toufiq made several foundational contributions that strengthened the core architecture of JIAK’s merchant and customer systems.

He has a remarkable ability to transform complexity into clarity. Whether refactoring legacy modules, establishing cleaner design patterns, or bringing structure to areas that needed a more scalable approach, his work always elevated the codebase and set new standards for maintainability. His problem-solving approach mirrors what I expect from top startup engineers: thoughtful root-cause analysis, disciplined execution, and the instinct to always leave systems in a better state than he found them.

Beyond backend engineering, Toufiq contributed across the stack—supporting infrastructure work, collaborating closely with DevOps, and helping maintain our monitoring and observability systems. His involvement ensured our services remained stable, predictable, and production-ready as we continued to scale.

Toufiq embodies a true startup-engineer mindset — proactive, resourceful, deeply curious, and unfazed by ambiguity. He collaborates exceptionally well across teams and brings a level of maturity and calmness that’s rare. His contributions to post-mortems, technical discussions, and architecture improvements were consistently well-structured, insightful, and action-oriented.

He is an engineer who takes ownership, thinks long-term, and operates with a builder’s mentality. Any team looking to add a thoughtful, reliable, and scalable-system-oriented backend engineer would be very fortunate to have him.

I strongly recommend Md Toufiqul Islam for any challenging backend or platform engineering role — he will raise the technical bar wherever he goes.
